unreal_log_summary: Using first-party procedural material recipe plan: unreal/Content/RaftSim/Rendering/first_party_procedural_material_recipes.json
Using production geospatial attachment ledger: physics/data/real_world/production_geospatial_attachment_ledger.json
Replaced broad opaque source-drape overlay plates with first-party feathered, terrain-lit microtiles that sample local water and vegetation masks per vertex.
Added first-party near-field water surface grain to the base river ribbon with higher mesh resolution, fine-ripple displacement, dark slicks, and subtle bright streaks.
Added first-party base water depth/current grading to the river ribbon with muted river-specific deep water, center current tongues, sediment threads, and sky-thread variation.
Muted South Fork and Pacuare foliage base tints, reduced generic proxy emissive lift, and lowered branch/frond leaf-tip highlights for first-party photographic foliage tone calibration.
Corrected the soft-lit terrain material emissive override to a low photographic lift so sun/skylight and mesh normals shape terrain proxies.
Raised the generated terrain luma floor, reduced terrain-feature overlay strength, and softened proxy terrain normals so coarse bank/canyon/rainforest meshes no longer collapse into black plates.
Added bounded terrain ambient fill at 0.16, below the rejected flat 0.42 terrain override, to keep coarse generated slopes readable without claiming final terrain lighting.

Added first-party base terrain material grain and microrelief directly to the generated terrain mesh so broad bank/canyon/rainforest surfaces have source-mask-aware mottle and small normal-scale relief without adding more overlay plates.

Rounded the first-party guide-seat foreground proxy by switching the visible bow, frame bar, tube seams, bow line, and oar shafts to cylinder geometry and shrinking the remaining flat floor/blade pieces.
Demoted terrain overlay striping by reducing terrain material facet, Landscape/Nanite scaffold, strata microband, and slope-occlusion counts, sizes, and color contrast.

Added a first-party source-drape smoothing pass that clamps bright source imagery, reduces aerial microtile density and blend weight, and lowers bank/rill/scaffold overlay density so source-derived terrain cues stop reading as hard painted bands.
Segmented wet-bank and source-detail ribbons by lowering their width, height, color gain, continuity, and secondary band count so waterline review cues do not read as continuous painted slabs.
Demoted near-frame wet-bank, shallow-water, and aeration scaffold lines so foreground review frames preserve the downstream river read.
Demoted procedural source-detail contour bands to zero active bands in the preview renderer while preserving narrower waterline/gravel ribbons for review cues.
Raised the bounded terrain readability fill from 0.16 to 0.22 after contour demotion, staying below the rejected flat 0.42 override while keeping dark proxy banks reviewable.

Added first-party fine twig canopy lace geometry to source-aware and dense biome foliage crowns so tree silhouettes break into smaller twig/leaflet clusters without claiming production foliage assets.

Saved all three generated preview maps with softened source-drape overlays, boulder-contact foam laces, guide-seat foliage setback and scale tuning, source-aware bank breakup texture patches, Guide-seat captures include proxy player-view context while river-eye captures hide the proxies for environment review. The images remain preview-only and cannot be called lifelike until production terrain/material/water/foliage assets, approved raft/oar foreground art, guide review, rights review, and performance evidence pass.
